# UX Requirements Quality Checklist: Full-Window WSPR Map Experience

**Purpose**: Validate clarity, completeness, consistency, and measurability of UX and interaction requirements before implementation.
**Created**: 2026-05-17
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are full-window map layout requirements complete for desktop and mobile-sized viewports? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-017]
- [x] CHK002 Are all required overlay controls documented, including callsign, Set Callsign, refresh countdown, base map selector, contact details, and version hash? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-019, Spec §FR-021, Spec §FR-025]
- [x] CHK003 Are localStorage callsign persistence requirements complete for first-time, returning, invalid saved value, and unavailable storage scenarios? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-016, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK004 Are base map layer requirements complete enough to identify included layers, attribution, usage constraints, and failure behavior? [Completeness, Spec §FR-021, Spec §FR-022, Spec §FR-024, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK005 Are contact details requirements complete for field coverage, panel open/close behavior, and stale selected contact behavior? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-020]
- [x] CHK006 Are refresh interaction requirements complete for automatic refresh, manual refresh, duplicate prevention, failure state, and countdown reset timing? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-015]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK007 Is “full visible browser window” defined with measurable layout criteria and exclusions for overlay controls? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001, Spec §SC-002]
- [x] CHK008 Is “subtly” for the version hash quantified or constrained enough to avoid conflicting visual interpretations? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-025, Spec §SC-011]
- [x] CHK009 Is “small countdown” specified with enough placement, readability, and interaction criteria for implementation consistency? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK010 Is “right-side overlay panel” specific enough about placement, behavior on narrow screens, and relationship to other overlays? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK011 Is “all WSPRNET/WSPR API payload fields” defined against a clear payload source and transformation boundary? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014, Spec Key Entities]
- [x] CHK012 Are OpenStreetMap Standard, OpenTopoMap, and OpenStreetMap Humanitarian requirements documented with inclusion criteria that can be applied consistently? [Clarity, Spec §FR-022, Spec Assumptions]
- [x] CHK013 Are “visible or accessible” attribution requirements specific enough to determine acceptable attribution presentation? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-024]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK014 Are callsign prompt requirements consistent between initial prompt, active overlay re-selection, invalid saved values, and persistent Set Callsign control? [Consistency, Spec §US1, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-019]
- [x] CHK015 Are refresh requirements consistent between the five-minute interval, countdown reset after completion or failure, and failure preservation behavior? [Consistency, Spec §US4, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-015]
- [x] CHK016 Are contact selection requirements consistent between removing the persistent contact list and requiring direct map-path selection? [Consistency, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-013]
- [x] CHK017 Are base map switching requirements consistent with preserving selected contact details and closing stale selected contacts after data refresh? [Consistency, Spec §FR-020, Spec §FR-023]
- [x] CHK018 Are version indicator requirements consistent between the bottom-left placement and other map attribution or base-layer information requirements? [Consistency, Spec §FR-024, Spec §FR-025]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK019 Are success criteria measurable for all primary UX goals, including setup, layout, refresh, map selection, base map switching, and version hash visibility? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001 through Spec §SC-011]
- [x] CHK020 Are subjective UX goals such as “subtle,” “clear,” and “primary focus” supported by objective criteria? [Measurability, Spec §US2, Spec §FR-025]
- [x] CHK021 Are interaction-count criteria defined for every user flow that promises low-friction operation? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-004, Spec §SC-010]
- [x] CHK022 Are timing criteria defined for data loading and refresh flows without over-specifying implementation mechanics? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-005, Spec §SC-006]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK023 Are primary user journeys covered for first-time callsign setup, returning-user bypass, full-window map use, base map switching, refresh, and contact inspection? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios]
- [x] CHK024 Are alternate flows covered for changing callsign, changing base map, manually refreshing, selecting another contact, and dismissing panels? [Coverage, Spec §US2, Spec §US3, Spec §US4, Spec §US5]
- [x] CHK025 Are recovery flows specified for dismissed callsign prompt, invalid saved callsign, refresh failure, base map layer failure, and stale selected contact? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK026 Are empty and loading states specified for no active callsign, no returned contacts, active refresh, and failed refresh? [Gap, Spec §US1, Spec §US4, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK027 Are mobile/narrow viewport scenarios specified for all overlays, not only general map use? [Coverage, Spec §FR-017, Spec Edge Cases]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK028 Are requirements defined for localStorage read/write failure or unavailable browser storage? [Gap, Spec §FR-003, Plan §Storage]
- [x] CHK029 Are requirements defined for version hash unavailability during build or startup? [Gap, Spec §FR-025, Spec Assumptions]
- [x] CHK030 Are requirements defined for attribution conflicts when the bottom-left version hash and map attribution compete for space? [Gap, Spec §FR-024, Spec §FR-025]
- [x] CHK031 Are requirements defined for overlapping overlay panels when callsign selection, contact details, base map selector, and refresh status could be active? [Gap, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-021]
- [x] CHK032 Are requirements defined for keyboard and assistive-technology access to map path selection without relying solely on pointer interaction? [Gap, Contract §Accessibility Requirements]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK033 Are accessibility requirements specified for every interactive overlay control and not only the general map controls? [Coverage, Contract §Accessibility Requirements]
- [x] CHK034 Are performance requirements complete for map interactions, overlay updates, and up to 1,000 displayed paths? [Completeness, Plan §Performance Goals]
- [x] CHK035 Are privacy requirements for storing the callsign in localStorage explicit enough for a public callsign context? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003, Plan §Storage]
- [x] CHK036 Are attribution and usage-limit requirements documented as constraints for all open base map layers? [Dependencies, Spec §FR-022, Spec §FR-024]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK037 Are assumptions about existing WSPR API behavior explicitly linked to requirements that depend on map-ready features and all contact fields? [Assumption, Spec §FR-014, Plan §Summary]
- [x] CHK038 Are dependencies on build or startup metadata for the version hash documented enough to avoid implementation ambiguity? [Dependency, Spec §FR-025, Spec Assumptions]
- [x] CHK039 Are assumptions about “same browser profile or device” persistence consistent across spec, plan, and data model? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, Plan §Storage, Data Model §CallsignPersistence]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK040 Is the boundary between stakeholder-facing requirements and implementation constraints clear for mandatory deck.gl usage? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-018, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK041 Are all UX requirements traceable from user stories to functional requirements and success criteria? [Traceability, Spec §User Scenarios, Spec §Functional Requirements, Spec §Success Criteria]
- [x] CHK042 Is there a documented requirement ID strategy for UI contract items that are not directly numbered in the spec? [Traceability, Contract §Test Expectations]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
